# LeanCTX — Reduce AI Coding Token Costs by 99%

> Open-source MCP server + shell hook that reduces Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and Windsurf token costs by up to 99%. Single Rust binary with 34 intelligent tools, 90+ CLI compression patterns, and zero telemetry. Works with 24 AI coding tools.

## What is LeanCTX?

LeanCTX (lean-ctx) is an open-source context compression system for AI coding tools. It sits transparently between your AI tool (Cursor, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf, Gemini CLI, etc.) and the LLM, compressing all context before it reaches the model. This reduces token usage by 60-99%, cuts API costs, and makes AI responses faster.

Install in 60 seconds: `npm install -g lean-ctx-bin && lean-ctx setup`

## How It Reduces Token Costs

1. **MCP Server**: 34 compression tools replace built-in file reads, shell commands, and code search. Uses tree-sitter AST parsing across 18 programming languages. Eight read modes: full, map, signatures, diff, aggressive, entropy, task, lines:N-M. Cached re-reads cost ~13 tokens.
2. **Shell Hook**: Intercepts 90+ CLI command patterns (git, npm, cargo, docker, kubectl, pip, go, tsc, curl) and compresses output by 60-95%.
3. **AI Tool Hooks**: One-command integration for 24 editors via `lean-ctx setup`. No workflow changes needed.
4. **Communication Protocols**: CEP (Cognitive Efficiency Protocol), CCP (Context Continuity Protocol), and TDD (Token Dense Dialect) teach the AI to respond more efficiently, saving 8-25% additional tokens.

## Real Token Savings

| Operation | Without lean-ctx | With lean-ctx | Saved |
|:---|---:|---:|:---:|
| File reads (cached) | 30,000 tokens | 195 tokens | 99% |
| git status/log/diff | 8,000 tokens | 2,400 tokens | 70% |
| ls / find | 6,400 tokens | 1,280 tokens | 80% |
| Test runners | 10,000 tokens | 1,000 tokens | 90% |
| curl (JSON) | 1,500 tokens | 165 tokens | 89% |
| **Typical session** | **~89,800** | **~10,620** | **88%** |

Active developers save **$30-100+ per month** on AI API costs.

## Scientific Compression Engine

The compression engine is powered by six research-backed algorithms:

1. **Predictive Surprise** — BPE cross-entropy scoring. Keeps tokens that surprise a language model; discards predictable boilerplate.
2. **Spectral Relevance** — PageRank + heat diffusion on the dependency graph. Propagates importance scores through code relationships.
3. **Boltzmann Allocation** — Statistical mechanics for token budgets. Distributes context capacity based on information temperature.
4. **Semantic Chunking** — Attention bridges identify natural break points in code. Preserves coherent logical units, not arbitrary line counts.
5. **MMR Deduplication** — Jaccard similarity across cached files. Eliminates redundant patterns without losing unique information.
6. **BPE Optimization** — Token-aligned encoding. Restructures output to minimize actual BPE token count, not just character count.

## Supported AI Coding Tools (24)

Cursor, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf, VS Code, Zed, OpenAI Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Cline, Roo Code, Google Antigravity, Pi Coding Agent, OpenCode, Aider, Amp, AWS Kiro, JetBrains IDEs, Amazon Q Developer, Qwen Code, Trae, Continue, Crush, Google Antigravity.

All configured automatically with `lean-ctx setup`.

## Key Facts

- Version: 2.21.1
- License: MIT (fully open source)
- Language: Rust (single binary, ~17 MB with tree-sitter, ~5.7 MB without)
- Install: `npm install -g lean-ctx-bin` or `cargo install lean-ctx` or `brew install lean-ctx`
- Setup: `lean-ctx setup` (auto-configures shell + all detected editors)
- Telemetry: Zero. None. Ever.
- Repository: https://github.com/yvgude/lean-ctx
- Crate: https://crates.io/crates/lean-ctx
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/lean-ctx-bin
- Website: https://leanctx.com
- Discord: https://discord.gg/pTHkG9Hew9

- [Home](https://leanctx.com/): Overview and quick start
- [How It Works](https://leanctx.com/how-it-works/): Data flow, compression pipeline, quality preservation
- [MCP Server](https://leanctx.com/mcp-server/): 34 compression tools documentation
- [Shell Hook](https://leanctx.com/shell-hook/): 90+ command patterns with before/after examples
- [Compatibility](https://leanctx.com/compatibility/): All 24 supported AI tools with setup commands
- [Benchmark](https://leanctx.com/benchmark/): Token savings measurement and verification
- [CLI Reference](https://leanctx.com/cli/): Terminal commands for humans
- [Protocols](https://leanctx.com/protocols/): CEP, CCP, and TDD communication protocols
- [Getting Started](https://leanctx.com/docs/getting-started/): Installation and setup guide
- [Configuration](https://leanctx.com/docs/configuration/): config.toml, CLI cache, environment variables
- [Tool API Reference](https://leanctx.com/docs/tools/): All 34 MCP tools documented
- [Intelligence Layer](https://leanctx.com/docs/intelligence-layer/): 6 scientific compression algorithms explained
- [Tree-sitter Engine](https://leanctx.com/docs/tree-sitter/): AST parsing for 18 languages
- [Quick Reference](https://leanctx.com/docs/quick-reference/): Daily workflow cheat sheet

## FAQ

**How much money does lean-ctx save?**
Active developers save $30-100+ per month on API costs. The exact amount depends on your AI tool, usage patterns, and pricing tier.

**Does lean-ctx work with Claude Code / Cursor / Copilot?**
Yes — lean-ctx supports 24 AI coding tools out of the box. Run `lean-ctx setup` and it auto-detects and configures all installed editors.

**Does it slow down my AI tool?**
No. lean-ctx adds <1ms overhead per operation. Most users report their AI tools feel faster because less data means faster LLM responses.

**Is it safe?**
Zero telemetry — no data collection, no analytics, no network requests, ever. Everything runs 100% locally. MIT-licensed and fully auditable.

**What's the difference between lean-ctx and RTK?**
lean-ctx is a hybrid architecture (shell hook + MCP server) while RTK is shell-hook only. lean-ctx offers 34 tools vs RTK's basic compression, supports 24 editors vs 3, has tree-sitter AST parsing for 18 languages, and zero telemetry (RTK has default-on telemetry).
